verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is the beta subunit of the F1 catalytic domain of mitochondrial ATP synthase. The protein is synthesized in the cytoplasm and translocated to the inner mitochondrial membrane, where it forms part of the F1 complex facing the matrix. It is not a transmembrane protein, has no GPI anchor, and has no documented plasma membrane association, surface cycling, or cell-state-induced ecto-display. No known surface-directed therapeutics target this protein. Contextual checks: no documented cancer / cell-death / activation ecto-pool; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool or cycling; no covalent or wash-resistant surface anchoring. The protein body does not reach the outer face of the plasma membrane.
